company_type: Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT) - Gaming, Hospitality, Entertainment & Leisure

dependencies: Dependence on tenants for substantially all revenues
Caesars and MGM comprise approximately 74% of total leasing revenues
Properties on Las Vegas Strip generated approximately 48% of revenues
Reliance on third-party golf resort operator (Cabot-Managed Properties)
risks: Concentration risk with Caesars (39% of annualized rent) and MGM (35% of annualized rent)
Geographic concentration risk - Las Vegas Strip properties
Gaming industry cyclicality and regulatory changes
Tenant financial performance directly impacts VICI's credit loss allowances

regulatory_risks: Extensive gaming regulation from state and county gaming authorities
Gaming license requirements for tenants and VICI as landlord
Suitability determinations for stockholders, officers, and directors by gaming authorities
Ownership limits - stockholders owning >5% may require licensing or suitability findings
VICI charter provides shares held by unsuitable investors subject to redemption
Required regulatory approvals can delay or prohibit property transfers
Gaming authorities can revoke, suspend, condition or limit licenses
Gaming compliance issues in one jurisdiction may trigger reviews in other jurisdictions
Corporate actions (acquisitions, financing, management changes) require gaming authority approval
REIT qualification requirements - must distribute 90% of taxable income annually
Environmental compliance laws (federal, state, local) for property ownership
Climate change laws and building performance standards (energy emissions reduction)
Twelve leased properties subject to energy benchmarking/building performance standards
New climate disclosure rules from SEC (pending litigation stay)
Tax law changes including OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax (15%)
Foreign currency regulations in Canada and UK
Insurance coverage requirements under lease agreements
